channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Is that it? IKEA's has a limited-edition merch range to mark its 40th birthday — but it's rather underwhelming
item.description: The Aurtiende range lets you express your IKEA love on your chest, or head — but I think the brand could have put a bit more effort in.

item.title: 'Long term, the only manually driven car will be the Tesla Roadster': Elon Musk says future lineup is going to be almost entirely autonomous
item.description: Tesla's CEO says that the only non-autonomous vehicle in the company's future line-up will be the Roadster.

item.title: ‘We Are Xbox’: read the memo defining Microsoft’s gaming future
item.description: Microsoft's new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has laid out a new strategy for what she describes as a "return of Xbox." In a joint memo with Xbox Chief Content Officer Matt Booty, Sharma and Booty lay out their vision for the future of Xbox that will be judged on daily active players and the priorities […]

item.title: Microsoft’s new Xbox chief is ‘reevaluating’ exclusive games
item.description: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma held an all-hands meeting this morning and laid out her strategy for a "return of Xbox" with employees. While rebranding Microsoft Gaming back to Xbox was part of the agenda, Sharma also revealed that Xbox is looking into "reevaluating" Xbox exclusive games and windowed releases of titles. It's a hot topic […]

item.title: 'Identity is the new battleground': Why your IT helpdesk is suddenly getting a lot of bizarre calls
item.description: Okta vishing attacks rely on impersonation and urgency, allowing attackers to manipulate support staff and quickly escalate into large-scale data breaches.

item.title: Don’t stop hiring humans — stop hiring the wrong humans, Artisan’s founder says
item.description: Artisan may be known for their bold “Stop Hiring Humans” campaign but the reality is every founder needs to assemble the right team if they want to scale.

item.title: OpenAI dégaine GPT-5.5 : « son modèle le plus intelligent et le plus intuitif à ce jour » pour battre Gemini
item.description: OpenAI annonce en grande pompe le déploiement de son nouveau modèle GPT-5.5 (et GPT-5.5 Pro) qu'on trouvera notamment sur ChatGPT et Codex. Objectif : battre Google Gemini.

item.title: Titanium Court mashes together genres and cultural references to tell a strange, funny tale
item.description: I would love to tell you everything about my favorite game of the year so far. But that would be doing a great disservice to Titanium Court. I'm not even sure I could explain it all, anyway.Titanium Court is a run-based game with elements of permanent progression, so it's technically a roguelite. However, you cannot really break Titanium Court like you can with Balatro. There are multiple ways to win a run, but you have to play by the rules. Gradually learning what those are — and how the game suddenly changes them — is a big part of what makes this so effective.I can at least break down the c...

item.title: If you think this Unitree G1 robot rollerblading is scary, wait until you see it iceskating — robot Winter Olympics can’t be far behind, and we should talk about robot mobility
item.description: Unitree drops a new video featuring a G1 with wheels instead of feet, and, in it, Unitree also equips its feet with skates.

item.title: Intelligence artificielle | OpenAI dévoile un nouveau modèle à l’encadrement renforcé
item.description: OpenAI a dévoilé jeudi un nouveau modèle d’intelligence artificielle (IA) qui se veut le plus avancé du marché selon plusieurs critères, et a promis d’accélérer encore le rythme, tout en vantant l’encadrement de son IA pour éviter les utilisations dangereuses.

item.title: Democrats want to ban ICE from turning warehouses into detention centers
item.description: A bill introduced by Rep. Rashida Tlaib (D-MI) would prohibit the Department of Homeland Security from converting warehouses and similar buildings into immigrant detention centers, an attempt to slow President Donald Trump's mass deportations campaign. The Ban Warehouse Detention Act would also forbid Immigration and Customs Enforcement from developing other "non-traditional" detention facilities. "ICE and […]...

item.title: Microsoft is making this first-ever move to cut workforce size amid the AI shift
item.description: Some employees are being offered a voluntary buyout, following recent mass layoffs in the tech industry. Microsoft is offering voluntary buyouts to select employees, it announced in a memo on Thursday, CNBC reported. The move is a first for the company, as the tech industry at large faces shifts in the era of artificial intelligence.

item.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, a more powerful engine for coding, science, and general work
item.description: The company is positioning its newest system as its strongest agentic coding model yet, as it faces pressure to keep pace with its rival Anthropic. OpenAI on Thursday released its most capable AI system, GPT-5.5, which the company says will enable a more powerful Codex coding agent. OpenAI is quick to say, however, that GPT-5.5 will power the widening set of general digital work tasks that Codex is capable of. The system is significantly better than previous releases at helping with scientific work, including creative aspects of generating new hypotheses and testing them. ...

item.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI ‘superapp’
item.description: OpenAI says its latest model offers increased capabilities across a broad variety of categories.

item.title: X is shutting down its Communities feature
item.description: X is closing its Communities feature in May, X Head of Product Nikita Bier has announced. Communities were introduced before Twitter was acquired and rebranded by Elon Musk, and act as a way for users to create, join and moderate public groups focused on a particular interest. Communities make it possible to follow a feed made up of only the people or subject matter you care about, but they haven't been used at the scale the social platform wanted."Communities had a great vision, but they were used by less than 0.4% of users — yet contributed to 80% of spam reports, financial scams, and malwar...

item.title: Spirit is broken
item.description: The bright yellow livery of Spirit Airlines may soon disappear from the skies. The country's seventh-largest airline has been in financial trouble for years: It hasn't turned a profit since 2019 and filed for bankruptcy twice in the last two years. Despite all that, its leaders predicted that the airline could exit bankruptcy and return […]

item.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 is here, and it's no potato: narrowly beats Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview on Terminal-Bench 2.0
item.description: After months of rumors and reports that OpenAI was developing a new, more powerful AI large language model for use in ChatGPT and through its application programming interface (API), allegedly codenamed "Spud" internally, the company has today unveiled its latest offering under the more formal name GPT-5.5.And to likely no one's surprise, it's hardly a "potato" in the disparaging sense of the word: GPT-5.5 retakes the lead for OpenAI in generally available LLMs, coming ahead of rivals Anthropic's and Google's latest public offerings, and even beating the private Anthropic Claude Mythos Preview...

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos breach was humiliating
item.description: Anthropic's tightly controlled rollout of Claude Mythos has taken an awkward turn. After spending weeks insisting the AI model is so capable at cybersecurity that it is too dangerous to release publicly, it appears the model fell into the wrong hands anyway. According to Bloomberg, a "small group of unauthorized users" has had access to […]

item.title: At 'AI Coachella,' Stanford Students Line Up to Learn From Silicon Valley Royalty
item.description: CS 153 has gone viral on the Palo Alto campus—and on X. Not everyone is happy about it.

item.title: Apple, Amazon join push for looser greenhouse emissions reporting
item.description: The Greenhouse Gas Protocol, a widely used international environmental standard for measuring and reporting emissions, is considering changes to how certain types of the emissions are reported. Advocates for the new guidance argue that the current rules make it too easy for businesses to overstate their commitments to environmentally friendly operations, such as being powered by renewable energy or making progress toward net-zero emissions. Today, some major tech companies joined a call pushing back against the new guidance, asking for the new reporting rules to be optional rather than require...

item.title: Meta to cut 10% of jobs, or 8,000 employees, report says
item.description: According to an internal memo, Meta plans to begin its sweeping layoffs on May 20.

item.title: Apple TV's upcoming For All Mankind spinoff Star City oozes Cold War-era paranoia
item.description:
Apple TV just dropped a real-deal trailer for Star City, after releasing a short teaser earlier this year. It's a spinoff of For All Mankind, but this new show examines the alt-history space race from the Soviet perspective. In other words, this is a trailer steeped in Cold War-era paranoia. Secret photos are snapped, phones are tapped and characters are disappeared, all set against the backdrop of space exploration. The vibe looks decidedly different from For All Mankind, despite the parent show occasionally dabbling in Russia-based espionage.
The vibe isn't the only shift here. Star City is...

item.title: Des « écailles de dragon » sur Mars ? Un rover de la Nasa vient de tomber sur bien plus qu’une curiosité
item.description: Sur Mars, le rover Curiosity rover a récemment mis au jour un paysage aussi étrange que spectaculaire : un sol couvert de motifs polygonaux évoquant des écailles de dragon. Derrière cette apparence insolite se cachent des processus géologiques complexes qui pourraient livrer de nouveaux indices...

item.title: OpenAI says its new GPT-5.5 model is more efficient and better at coding
item.description: OpenAI just announced its new GPT-5.5 model, which the company calls its "smartest and most intuitive to use model yet, and the next step toward a new way of getting work done on a computer." OpenAI just released GPT-5.4 last month, but says that the new GPT-5.5 "excels" at tasks like writing and debugging code, […]

item.title: What is the release date for Marshals: A Yellowstone Story episode 9 on CBS and Paramount+?
item.description: Last week finally saw the arrival of some truly high-stakes drama. But when does Marshals: A Yellowstone Story episode 9 hit CBS and Paramount+?

item.title: This free NASA tool lets you make a personalized satellite photo —and it's the most fun you'll have on Earth Day
item.description: NASA’s latest tool lets you spell your name in the Earth’s most unusually shaped features.

item.title: 'Just use the manual': I followed ChatGPT's PC-building instructions, and all I got was a huge headache and a schooling on prompting
item.description: Is AI the right companion when building a PC for the first time? Here's what I found out when I tried it.

item.title: Ikea’s new inflatable chair doesn’t look like an inflatable chair
item.description: Ikea shared a sneak preview of three pieces from a new experimental collection, set to be fully revealed at an annual company event on May 13th. One of the pieces is an inflatable chair that looks like a far cry from the cheap and lumpy inflatable furniture popularized in the '90s. This isn't the first […]

item.title: This Buc-ee’s candy is going viral—and resellers are already flipping it for 4 times the price
item.description: Social media loves the size, but the nutrition label is getting just as much attention. Thanks to social media, a new legion of fans is discovering something new to love at Buc-ee’s: the OverBite.

item.title: 'Humans being optional': Gartner says robots will dominate workload handling in 50% of new warehouses by 2030
item.description: Warehouses are evolving into automated environments where AI and robotics handle operations, reducing human involvement and increasing system adaptability.

item.title: 'An AI-led defense strategy that's overseen by humans': Google is introducing more agents to its 'full AI stack' to allow AI security at 'infinite scale'
item.description: Three new agents introduced at Google Cloud Next, which are soon to be released.

item.title: Great news, the Moto G Stylus is no longer teeming with bloatware
item.description: The 2026 edition of Motorola's stylus phone is plenty appealing. My review unit is a charming lilac color, has a pleasantly textured back panel, and includes a MicroSD slot and what might be the last surviving headphone jack on a mainstream phone sold in North America. The namesake stylus is no longer just a fancy […]

item.title: I've been a Spotify subscriber for 17 years — here are the top 5 plug-ins I recommend for the music streamer
item.description: Spotify is better with extensions — and these 5 in particular can help you make the most of the audio streaming platform.

item.title: UleFone RugKing 5 Pro review: While this rugged phone built to a price, it does have some virtues to offset the vices
item.description: The UleFone RugKing 5 Pro is a robust device with decent battery life, but built to a price specification.

item.title: Nintendo teases they'd 'like to adhere' to the Super Mario Galaxy Movie's lore in future games — including Princess Peach's backstory
item.description: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie could inform future Nintendo games, despite the mixed response from audiences.

item.title: J’ai testé le TV Samsung 55S99H : le QD‑OLED 55 pouces le plus ambitieux de Samsung en 2026
item.description: Le Samsung TQ55S99H est la déclinaison 55 pouces du nouveau fleuron QD‑OLED de la marque coréenne pour 2026. Visant un public exigeant à la recherche d’une image spectaculaire, d’un design à forte personnalité et des performances de haut niveau en jeu vidéo, je l’ai testé et en voici mes impressions.

item.title: Microsoft brings Xbox back, scraps Microsoft Gaming
item.description: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has had a busy week. Sharma held an internal town hall with Xbox employees today, just days after announcing an Xbox Game Pass price cut, commenting on the status of the Xbox mobile store, and teasing a mysterious Xbox and Discord partnership. One of the items on the agenda for the […]
